blocks.whenToUse: Narrative explanation, analysis, or framing. Supports a light markdown subset in content: "- " bullet lines and **bold** (bold is automatically rendered as a highlighted accent by the component). Great for analyst commentary as bullet points.
spec: { "type": "text", "id": "...", "content": "- **Data Center** at **$47.5B** is ~**78%** of total\n- **Gaming** recovered to **$10.4B** (+14% YoY)" }

example.content: Correlation measures how two assets move together.

blocks.whenToUse: A row of key metrics / headline numbers the user should see at a glance.
spec: { "type": "kpi_row", "id": "...", "items": [ { "label": "...", "value": "...", "tone": "positive|neutral|negative" }, ... ] }

blocks.whenToUse: Trends, comparisons, correlation matrices, or composition. Pick the kind that fits the data.
spec: Pick "kind" to fit the data; ONLY these kinds render:

UNITS — NEVER drop the unit. When values carry a currency/unit, set "value_prefix" and/or "value_suffix" on the chart so every label and tooltip shows it. Money in trillions: "value_prefix":"$","value_suffix":"T" (so 3.35 shows as $3.35T). Percentages: "value_suffix":"%". Billions: "value_suffix":"B". Keep the raw numbers in "values"/"items" plain (e.g. 3.35, not "$3.35T") — the unit lives ONLY in value_prefix/value_suffix.
- line | bar | hbar | area | stacked | combo (CATEGORICAL x-axis) -> you MUST give "x_categories" (the real labels) and each series as PLAIN NUMBERS aligned to them. NEVER use bare 0,1,2 as x.

{ "type": "chart", "title": "...", "chart": { "kind": "bar", "x_label": "Segment", "y_label": "USD B", "value_prefix": "$", "value_suffix": "B", "x_categories": ["Data Center", "Gaming", "Auto"], "series": [ { "name": "FY2024", "values": [47.5, 10.4, 1.1] }, { "name": "FY2023", "values": [15.0, 9.1, 0.9] } ] } }
(note the value_prefix "$" + value_suffix "B" — labels render as $47.5B. Always add the unit this way when the data has one.)
(values[i] pairs with x_categories[i]; one series per group, e.g. one per year. For combo, add "kind":"bar"|"line" per series.)
Do NOT include any "color" or styling field. Emit data only (name + values) — all colors, palette, and styling are applied automatically by the component. Styling is never your concern.
- scatter (numeric x vs y / correlation) -> series with [x, y] pairs (no x_categories).
{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "scatter", "series": [ { "name": "...", "values": [ [x, y], ... ] } ] } }
- heatmap (correlation matrix, confusion matrix, any grid of values) -> labels + a 2D "matrix" (rows = y_labels, cols = x_labels):
{ "type": "chart", "title": "...", "chart": { "kind": "heatmap", "x_labels": ["A","B"], "y_labels": ["A","B"], "matrix": [ [1, 0.3], [0.3, 1] ] } }
- pie | donut | treemap | sunburst (composition / share / hierarchy) -> use "items":

{ "type": "chart", "title": "...", "chart": { "kind": "treemap", "value_prefix": "$", "value_suffix": "T", "items": [ { "label": "Apple", "value": 3.35 }, { "label": "Microsoft", "value": 3.30 } ] } }  (renders tiles as "Apple $3.35T")
- waterfall (P&L bridge / cash-flow walk / variance) -> use "items" where each value is the DELTA (+/-):
{ "type": "chart", "title": "...", "chart": { "kind": "waterfall", "items": [ { "label": "Start", "value": 100 }, { "label": "Sales", "value": 30 }, { "label": "Costs", "value": -20 } ] } }
- candlestick (OHLC price) -> "x_categories" (dates) + "candles" of [open, close, low, high]:
- boxplot (distribution / quartiles) -> "x_categories" + "boxes" of [min, q1, median, q3, max]:
{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "boxplot", "x_categories": ["Fund A","Fund B"], "boxes": [ [-5,2,6,9,15], [-2,3,5,8,12] ] } }
- bubble (x vs y with size) -> series with [x, y, size] triples:
{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "bubble", "x_label": "Risk", "y_label": "Return", "series": [ { "name": "Funds", "values": [ [8,12,500], [14,18,1200] ] } ] } }
- sankey | graph (flows / network) -> "nodes" + "links":

{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "sankey", "nodes": [ {"name":"Revenue"}, {"name":"COGS"}, {"name":"Gross Profit"} ], "links": [ {"source":"Revenue","target":"COGS","value":40}, {"source":"Revenue","target":"Gross Profit","value":60} ] } }

- flow (a process / decision flowchart, laid out left-to-right with arrows) -> "nodes" + directed "links". Optionally give each node a "level" (0,1,2…) to force its column, and each link a "label" (e.g. "Yes"/"No"):

{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "flow", "nodes": [ {"name":"Start"}, {"name":"Review"}, {"name":"Approve"}, {"name":"Reject"} ], "links": [ {"source":"Start","target":"Review"}, {"source":"Review","target":"Approve","label":"Yes"}, {"source":"Review","target":"Reject","label":"No"} ] } }
- histogram (frequency) -> like bar: "x_categories" (bins) + one series of counts.
- timeseries (interactive time trend with zoom/pan) -> like line: "x_categories" (dates) + series of numbers. Best for many points over time.
- rose (nightingale / 'pizza' chart — share with variable-radius slices) -> use "items": [ { "label", "value" } ].
- polar (categorical bars around a circle) -> "x_categories" + series of numbers (like bar).
- parallel (compare items across many dimensions) -> "x_categories" = dimension names; one series per item with "values" aligned to them.
{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "parallel", "x_categories": ["P/E","Margin","Growth"], "series": [ { "name": "AAPL", "values": [28, 25, 8] }, { "name": "MSFT", "values": [34, 42, 14] } ] } }
- themeriver (streamgraph of categories over time; keep to a handful of time points) -> "x_categories" (time) + one series per category with "values" aligned to them.
- tree | mindmap | org (hierarchy / mind map / org chart) -> a single "root" node with nested "children":
{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "tree", "root": { "name": "Apple", "children": [ { "name": "Products", "children": [ {"name":"iPhone"}, {"name":"Mac"} ] }, { "name": "Services" } ] } } }
(use "mindmap" for a radial layout, "org" for top-down. Keep to a readable number of nodes.)
- scatter3d | bar3d | line3d (THREE numeric axes; keep to a handful of points) -> series with [x, y, z] triples:
{ "type": "chart", "chart": { "kind": "scatter3d", "x_label": "Risk", "y_label": "Return", "series": [ { "name": "Funds", "values": [ [8,12,500], [14,18,1200] ] } ] } }
For a correlation matrix you MUST use kind "heatmap" with "matrix" (never a line/bar series).

blocks.whenToUse: Tabular breakdown or side-by-side comparison of rows and columns.
Buttons to RE-VISUALIZE the SAME data already shown as a different supported chart kind. Each button's label is sent back as the next prompt.
spec: { "type": "action_row", "buttons": [ { "label": "Show as <supported chart kind>", "intent": "..." }, ... ] }

VISUALS ONLY — buttons MUST only offer to redraw the data ALREADY shown using a different supported chart kind (e.g. "Show as bar chart", "View as treemap", "Show as pie", "View as horizontal bars", "Show as line trend").

NEVER propose actions that require data you may not have: no new tickers/companies, no new time periods, no "compare X vs Y", no growth/peers/forecasts, no "explain ...". Only chart-type changes of the current data.

blocks.whenToUse: Photo, diagram, illustration, or icon via an https URL or data URI.
spec: { "type": "image", "id": "...", "title": "...", "src": "https://... OR data:image/png;base64,...", "alt": "accessible description", "caption": "optional caption", "fit": "contain|cover" }

blocks.whenToUse: Prominent headline metric cards — a few key figures shown large (label + value + optional subtext + tone).
spec: { "type": "stat_card", "items": [ { "label": "...", "value": "...", "subtext": "optional", "tone": "success|warning|danger|info|default" }, ... ] }

blocks.whenToUse: Percentage / progress bars (value vs max) — margins, allocations, % of target.
spec: { "type": "progress", "items": [ { "label": "...", "value": 44.1, "max": 100, "tone": "default|success|warning|danger|info" }, ... ] }
"tone" is SEMANTIC meaning only (good/at-risk/bad) — NEVER a color. The component owns all colors.

blocks.whenToUse: A row of small status pills (label + tone) — flags, statuses, tags.
spec: { "type": "badge_row", "items": [ { "label": "...", "tone": "default|success|warning|danger|info" }, ... ] }
